title: Webera albituba
taxonomy: Webera albituba is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. It is native to Southeast Asia, specifically in countries such as Malaysia and Thailand. The classification of this plant places it in the genus Webera, which is a group of plants within the family Acanthaceae. Webera albituba is further classified as belonging to the order Lamiales, which includes other flowering plants such as mint and snapdragons. The classification of Webera albituba is still being studied and updated by botanists and taxonomists.

intro: Webera albituba is a genus of plants in the family Menthaceae, commonly known as the Weber's mint or white-leaved mint. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in relieving digestive issues, reducing fever, and promoting wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Webera albituba include essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, such as limonene, menthone, and linalool, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
